name: Plum Tomato Tart
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Thyme, fresh, or 1/2 teaspoon dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Set oven rack to the upper position and preheat to 400°F.
2. Lightly coat a baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray or line with parchment paper.
3. In a small bowl, whisk together the egg white and 2 tablespoons olive oil.
4. Lay a sheet of phyllo on the prepared baking sheet and lightly coat the surface with the egg-white mixture. Sprinkle with 1 teaspoon breadcrumbs. Repeat this step, layering 4 more sheets of phyllo on top and brush with egg-white mixture.
5. To form an edge to the tart, carefully roll over the edges toward the center, using the blade of a knife to help you get started.
6. With a rubber spatula, spread mustard over the surface of the dough and sprinkle with cheese. (The tart can be prepared ahead to this point. Wrap and freeze for up to 2 months. Do not thaw before continuing.)
7. Arrange tomato slices on top in 5 rows of 8 slices each.
8. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown. Let cool in the pan for 5 minutes.
9. In a small bowl, combine the remaining tablespoon olive oil, parsley, garlic and thyme.
10. With your fingers or a fork, dab some of the herb mixture onto each tomato slice.
11. Slide the tart onto a serving platter or, if you wish to serve bite-sized appetizers, slide it onto a cutting board and with a sharp knife or pizza cutter, cut the tart into squares between the tomato slices.
12. Serve warm or at room temperature.
32 calories per piece: 1 g protein, 1 g fat, 4 g carbohydrate; 40 mg sodium; 1 mg cholesterol.
